steps.title: Relate tangent to the missing leg
steps.content:
Angle $Y$ is a right angle, so $XY$ and $YZ$ are the legs. Relative to angle $Z$, side $XY$ is opposite and side $YZ$ is adjacent. Since $YZ=28$, use tangent:
$$\tan 37^\circ=\frac{XY}{28}$$
$$XY=28\tan 37^\circ$$

steps.title: Write the area expression
steps.content: The area of a right triangle is one-half the product of its legs. Substitute $YZ=28$ and the expression for $XY$: $$\frac{1}{2}(28)(28\tan 37^\circ)=392\tan 37^\circ$$ The coefficient of $\tan 37^\circ$ is the value of $k$. The answer is 392.
